songs.title: ...Ready for It?
songs.lyrics:
Knew he was a killer first time that I saw him
Wondered how many girls he had loved and left haunted
But if he's a ghost, then I can be a phantom
Holdin' him for ransom, some
Some boys are tryin' too hard, he don't try at all, though
Younger than my exes, but he act like such a man, so
I see nothin' better, I keep him forever
Like a vendetta-ta

I, I, I see how this is gon' go
Touch me and you'll never be alone
I-Island breeze and lights down low
No one has to know

In the middle of the night, in my dreams
You should see the things we do, baby, mmm
In the middle of the night, in my dreams
I know I'm gonna be with you, so I take my time
Are you ready for it?

Knew I was a robber first time that he saw me
Stealin' hearts and runnin' off and never sayin' sorry
But if I'm a thief, then he can join the heist and
We'll move to an island and
And he can be my jailer, Burton to this Taylor
Every love I've known in comparison is a failure
I forget their names now, I'm so very tame now
Never be the same now, now

I, I, I see how this is gon' go (Go)
Touch me and you'll never be alone
I-Island breeze and lights down low (Low)
No one has to know (No one has to know)

In the middle of the night, in my dreams (Yeah)
You should see the things we do (We do), baby, mmm (Mmm)
In the middle of the night (The night) in my dreams (My dreams)
I know I'm gonna be with you, so I take my time
Are you ready for it? Oh
Are you ready for it?

Baby, let the games begin
Let the games begin
Let the games begin, ah-ah
Baby, let the games begin
Let the games begin
Let the games begin

I, I, I see how this is gon' go
Touch me and you'll never be alone
I-Island breeze and lights down low
No one has to know

In the middle of the night (Night), in my dreams (My dreams)
You should see the things we do (We do), baby (Baby), mmm (Yeah)
In the middle of the night, in my dreams (My dreams)
I know I'm gonna be with you, so I take my time (Yeah)
In the middle of the night

Baby, let the games begin
Let the games begin
Let the games begin
Are you ready for it?
Baby, let the games begin
Let the games begin
Let the games begin
Are you ready for it?

vocab.definition: a ghostly apparition or a person who is elusive or mysterious
vocab.lyric_snippet: But if he's a ghost, then I can be a phantom
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe herself as a mysterious and elusive person, mirroring the subject's ghostly nature.

vocab.definition: a long-standing grudge or feud, often driven by a desire for revenge
vocab.lyric_snippet: Like a vendetta-ta
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe a romantic relationship as a long-standing and intense feud, with a sense of obsession.

vocab.definition: a person who has control or authority over someone or something
vocab.lyric_snippet: And he can be my jailer, Burton to this Taylor
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe a romantic partner as someone who has control over her, with a sense of playfulness and intimacy.

vocab.definition: a daring or cunning theft, often involving a group of people
vocab.lyric_snippet: But if I'm a thief, then he can join the heist
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe a romantic relationship as a daring and exciting adventure, with a sense of playfulness.

vocab.definition: lacking excitement or passion, often used to describe someone or something that has lost its original energy or enthusiasm
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm so very tame now
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe herself as someone who has lost her original energy and enthusiasm, often due to past relationships.

vocab.definition: a person or thing that has not succeeded or achieved their goals
vocab.lyric_snippet: Every love I've known in comparison is a failure
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe past relationships as unsuccessful or unfulfilling, with a sense of regret and longing.

vocab.definition: extremely impressive or exciting; used to describe something or someone as being exceptionally talented or skilled.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You so dope, don't overdose
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'dope' to describe someone who is exceptionally talented or skilled, in this case, her partner.

vocab.definition: to come before something else in time or order.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I got a reputation, girl, that don't precede me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'precede' to describe how her reputation doesn't come before her in the sense that it defines her.

vocab.definition: a person's image or character as presented to the public.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I got a bad boy persona, that's what they like
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'persona' to describe the image or character she presents to the public, in this case, a 'bad boy' persona.

vocab.definition: small pieces of something, especially a hard substance like wood or stone.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Both sprung, I got issues and chips on both of my shoulders
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'chips' to describe small pieces of something, in this case, emotional baggage or scars.

vocab.definition: a feeling of suspicion or distrust, especially without any real reason.
vocab.lyric_snippet: For all my flaws, paranoia and insecurities
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'paranoia' to describe a feeling of suspicion or distrust, in this case, her own insecurities.

vocab.definition: feelings of uncertainty or self-doubt.
vocab.lyric_snippet: For all my flaws, paranoia and insecurities
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'insecurities' to describe feelings of uncertainty or self-doubt, in this case, her own flaws.

vocab.definition: to move something quickly, especially with a lot of energy.
vocab.lyric_snippet: We do the most, I'm in the Ghost like I'm whippin' a boat
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'whippin'' to describe moving something quickly, in this case, a boat.

songs.title: I Did Something Bad
songs.lyrics:
I never trust a narcissist, but they love me
So I play 'em like a violin
And I make it look, oh, so easy
'Cause for every lie I tell them, they tell me three
This is how the world works
Now all he thinks about is me

I can feel the flames on my skin
Crimson red paint on my lips
If a man talks shit, then I owe him nothin'
I don't regret it one bit 'cause he had it comin'

They say I did somethin' bad
Then why's it feel so good?

But why's it feel so good?
Most fun I ever had
And I'd do it over and over and over again if I could
It just felt so good, good

And I let them think they saved me
They never see it comin', what I do next
This is how the world works
You gotta leave before you get left

I can feel the flames on my skin
He says, "Don't throw away a good thing"
But if he drops my name, then I owe him nothin'
And if he spends my change, then he had it comin'

vocab.definition: A person who has an excessive love for or admiration of themselves, often to the point of self-obsession.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I never trust a narcissist, but they love me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is self-absorbed and loves themselves, but still has a fascination with her.

vocab.definition: A man who has many romantic relationships or affairs, often with a sense of charm or charisma.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I never trust a playboy, but they love me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a man who is charming and has many romantic relationships, but is not trustworthy.

vocab.definition: Long-handled tools with a pointed end, often used for spearing or piercing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They got their pitchforks and proof, their receipts and reasons
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a mob or a group of people who are angry and want to attack or punish someone.

vocab.definition: Evidence or proof of something, often in the form of documents or records.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They got their pitchforks and proof, their receipts and reasons
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the evidence or proof that people use to accuse or condemn someone.

vocab.definition: People who are believed to have supernatural powers or to be involved in witchcraft.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They're burning all the witches, even if you aren't one
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe people who are being accused or persecuted for something they are not guilty of.

vocab.definition: Evidence or proof of something, often in the form of documents or records.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They got their pitchforks and proof, their receipts and reasons
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the evidence or proof that people use to accuse or condemn someone.

vocab.definition: Relating to or characteristic of a narcissist, often used to describe someone who is self-absorbed or self-obsessed.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I never trust a narcissist, but they love me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is self-absorbed and loves themselves, but still has a fascination with her.

vocab.definition: A person's charm or attractiveness, often used to describe someone who is persuasive or compelling.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I never trust a playboy, but they love me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a man who is charming and has many romantic relationships.

vocab.definition: extremely foolish or unreasonable; wildly enthusiastic
vocab.lyric_snippet: Don't blame me, love made me crazy
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'crazy' to describe the effect love has on her, implying an all-consuming and potentially destructive passion.

vocab.definition: a place of beauty, happiness, or bliss
vocab.lyric_snippet: In the darkest little paradise
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'paradise' to describe a romantic relationship, suggesting a place of comfort and happiness.

vocab.definition: past tense of 'pace', to walk quickly and regularly
vocab.lyric_snippet: Shaking, pacin', I just need you
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pacin'' to describe her anxious and restless state, emphasizing her emotional turmoil.

vocab.definition: things that are used or treated as if they were toys
vocab.lyric_snippet: Just playthings for me to use
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'playthings' to describe the people she uses for her own pleasure, implying a lack of emotional investment.

vocab.definition: an all-consuming and often unhealthy interest or desire
vocab.lyric_snippet: Halo hidin' my obsession
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'obsession' to describe her all-consuming passion for the person she loves, suggesting a loss of control.

vocab.definition: a plant that causes an itchy rash when touched
vocab.lyric_snippet: I once was poison ivy, but now, I'm your daisy
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'poison ivy' as a metaphor for her past self, suggesting a toxic and hurtful nature, which she has since changed.

vocab.definition: a type of flower, often associated with innocence and purity
vocab.lyric_snippet: I once was poison ivy, but now, I'm your daisy
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'daisy' to describe her current self, suggesting a newfound innocence and purity.

vocab.definition: a ring of light around the head of a person, often symbolizing divine or spiritual presence
vocab.lyric_snippet: Halo hidin' my obsession
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'halo' as a metaphor for the protective and all-consuming nature of her love, suggesting a sense of divine or spiritual presence.

vocab.definition: requiring careful and gentle handling; easily damaged or hurt
vocab.lyric_snippet: Cause I know that it's delicate (Delicate)
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the fragile nature of their relationship, implying that it needs to be handled with care.

vocab.definition: relaxed and calm; not worried or anxious
vocab.lyric_snippet: Is it chill that you're in my head?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her relaxed and nonchalant attitude towards the other person being in her thoughts.

vocab.definition: a faint or repeated sound; a memory or feeling that remains after something has finished
vocab.lyric_snippet: Echoes of your footsteps on the stairs
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the lingering memory of the other person's presence, creating a sense of intimacy.

vocab.definition: to imagine or act as if something is true, even though it is not
vocab.lyric_snippet: I pretend you're mine all the damn time
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her fantasy of being in a relationship with the other person, highlighting her desire and longing.

vocab.definition: a large and luxurious house, especially one that is impressive or grand
vocab.lyric_snippet: Handsome, you're a mansion with a view
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the other person as someone who is impressive and desirable, with a sense of grandeur.

vocab.definition: a sight or prospect that can be seen from a particular place
vocab.lyric_snippet: Handsome, you're a mansion with a view
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the other person's attractive and desirable qualities, much like a beautiful view.

vocab.definition: exactly right or correct; flawless

vocab.context: Taylor uses 'perfect' to describe a crime as meticulous and well-planned, implying a sense of calculated malice.

vocab.definition: the idea that people get what they deserve, often in the form of punishment or reward
vocab.lyric_snippet: all I think about is karma
vocab.context: Taylor references karma as a concept of retribution, suggesting that she believes she will be avenged for past wrongs.

vocab.definition: a realm or territory ruled by a king or queen
vocab.lyric_snippet: I don't like your kingdom keys
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'kingdom' to describe a domain or territory, possibly referencing her own perceived loss of control or power.

vocab.definition: the last moment before something happens
vocab.lyric_snippet: But I got smarter, I got harder in the nick of time
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'nick' to describe a moment of crisis or turning point, where she rose up and became stronger.

vocab.definition: a woman who acts in plays, films, or television shows
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'll be the actress starrin' in your bad dreams
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'actress' to describe herself as a performer or manipulator, suggesting that she will be the one to haunt her enemies' nightmares.

vocab.definition: A reference to F. Scott Fitzgerald's novel 'The Great Gatsby', describing someone who is extravagant and flashy.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Feelin' so Gatsby for that whole year
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the lavish and extravagant lifestyle of the partygoers.

vocab.definition: A decorative lighting fixture, typically a large, ornate one.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Bass beat rattling the chandelier
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the luxurious and opulent atmosphere of the party.

vocab.definition: A public display or procession, often with music and decorations.
vocab.lyric_snippet: So why'd you have to rain on my parade?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the excitement and celebration of the party, which is ruined by the person's actions.

vocab.definition: Made or repaired, especially to restore something to its original condition.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And so I took an axe to a mended fence
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the effort she put into repairing the friendship, which is now being torn down.

vocab.definition: To deceive or cheat someone, often by using cunning or cleverness.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Friends don't try to trick you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's manipulative behavior, trying to deceive her.

vocab.definition: To confuse or distort someone's thoughts or perceptions.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Get you on the phone and mind-twist you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's manipulative behavior, trying to confuse and distort her thoughts.

vocab.definition: Untrustworthy or deceitful, often in a way that is sneaky or underhanded.
vocab.lyric_snippet: If only you weren't so shady
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's untrustworthy and deceitful behavior.

vocab.definition: A phrase used to describe a situation where two people have different versions of the truth, often with no clear resolution.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They don't care about the he-said, she-said
vocab.context: Taylor uses this phrase to describe the way people often focus on gossip and rumors, rather than the truth.

songs.title: Call It What You Want
songs.lyrics:
My castle crumbled overnight
I brought a knife to a gunfight
They took the crown, but it's alright
All the liars are calling me one
Nobody's heard from me for months
I'm doin' better than I ever was
'Cause

My baby's fit like a daydream
Walkin' with his head down
I'm the one he's walking to
So call it what you want, yeah
Call it what you want to
My baby's fly like a jet stream
High above the whole scene
Loves me like I'm brand new
So call it what you want, yeah
Call it what you want to

All my flowers grew back as thorns
Windows boarded up after the storm
He built a fire just to keep me warm
All the drama queens taking swings
All the jokers dressing up as kings
They fade to nothing when I look at him

And I know I make the same mistakes every time
Bridges burn, I never learn
At least I did one thing right
I did one thing right
I'm laughing with my lover, makin' forts under covers
Trust him like a brother

Starry eyes sparkin' up my darkest night

Walkin' with his head down

High above the whole scene

I want to wear his initial on a chain 'round my neck
Chain 'round my neck
Not because he owns me
But 'cause he really knows me
Which is more than they can say, I
I recall late November
Holding my breath, slowly, I said
"You don't need to save me
But would you run away with me?"
Yes (Would you run away?)

I'm the one he's walking to

High above the whole scene

vocab.definition: past tense of crumble, to fall apart or break down
vocab.lyric_snippet: My castle crumbled overnight
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a sudden and dramatic downfall, likely referring to her reputation or personal life.

vocab.definition: people who tell lies
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the liars are calling me one
vocab.context: Taylor refers to people who are spreading false information or rumors about her, and she's being labeled as one of them.

vocab.definition: a fast-moving stream of air in the upper atmosphere
vocab.lyric_snippet: My baby's fly like a jet stream
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her partner's speed and agility, comparing him to a fast-moving stream of air.

vocab.definition: women who are overly dramatic or attention-seeking
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the drama queens taking swings
vocab.context: Taylor refers to people who are causing trouble or drama, likely in her personal life or public image.

vocab.definition: people who are foolish or annoying
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the jokers dressing up as kings
vocab.context: Taylor refers to people who are pretending to be something they're not, likely in her personal life or public image.

vocab.definition: a phrase meaning relationships or opportunities are ruined
vocab.lyric_snippet: Bridges burn, I never learn
vocab.context: Taylor uses this phrase to describe how she's repeatedly made the same mistakes, causing relationships or opportunities to be ruined.

vocab.definition: small, enclosed spaces, often for protection or comfort
vocab.lyric_snippet: Makin' forts under covers
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a cozy and intimate setting with her partner, likely a metaphor for their relationship.

vocab.definition: the first letter of a person's name
vocab.lyric_snippet: I want to wear his initial on a chain 'round my neck
vocab.context: Taylor wants to wear her partner's initial as a symbol of their relationship, likely a token of affection.

songs.title: New Year's Day
songs.lyrics:
There's glitter on the floor after the party
Girls carryin’ their shoes down in the lobby
Candle wax and Polaroids on the hardwood floor
You and me from the night before, but

Don't read the last page
But I stay when you're lost and I’m scared and you're turnin' away
I want your midnights
But I'll be cleanin' up bottles with you on New Year's Day

You squeeze my hand three times in the back of the taxi
I can tell that it's gonna be a long road
I'll be there if you’re the toast of the town, babe
Or if you strike out and you’re crawlin' home

Don’t read the last page
But I stay when it's hard or it's wrong or we're makin’ mistakes
I want your midnights
But I'll be cleanin' up bottles with you on New Year's Day

Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
And I will hold on to you

Please, don't ever become a stranger
Whose laugh I could recognize anywhere
Please, don't ever become a stranger
Whose laugh I could recognize anywhere

There's glitter on the floor after the party
Girls carryin' their shoes down in the lobby
Candle wax and Polaroids on the hardwood floor
You and me forevermore

Don't read the last page
But I stay when it's hard or it's wrong or we're makin' mistakes
I want your midnights
But I'll be cleanin' up bottles with you on New Year's Day

Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you
And I will hold on to you (Hold on)

Please, don't ever become a stranger
(To the memories, they will hold on to you)
Whose laugh I could recognize anywhere
(Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you)
Please, don't ever become a stranger
(Hold on to the memories, they will hold on to you)
Whose laugh I could recognize anywhere
(And I will hold on to you)

vocab.definition: the darkest or most difficult times of the day or night, often used to describe a period of time when someone is feeling their most vulnerable or emotional.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I want your midnights
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'midnights' to describe the late hours of the night when she's most emotionally connected to the person she's singing about.

vocab.definition: past tense of 'make', used to describe the act of creating or doing something, often in a casual or informal context.
vocab.lyric_snippet: we're makin' mistakes
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'makin'' to describe the process of making mistakes and learning from them in a relationship.

vocab.definition: past tense of 'crawl', used to describe the act of moving slowly and painfully, often in a humiliating or degrading situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: you're crawlin' home
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'crawlin'' to describe the humiliation and shame of being rejected or abandoned by someone.

vocab.definition: a person who is admired or celebrated for their achievements, often in a public or social context.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'll be there if you're the toast of the town, babe
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'toast' to describe the person she's singing about as someone who is admired and celebrated by others.

vocab.definition: a public area or space, often in a building or establishment, where people can gather or wait.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Girls carryin' their shoes down in the lobby
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'lobby' to describe the public area where people are gathered after a party.

vocab.definition: a type of instant camera or photograph, often used to describe a casual or informal snapshot.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Candle wax and Polaroids on the hardwood floor
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'Polaroids' to describe the casual and informal photographs taken at a party.
